declarations.description: An editable `book:Bookmark` form element.
declarations.name: JeswrBookmarkForm

members.name: shapeTurtle
members.description: The model's SHACL shape graph as inline Turtle.

members.name: applyFormDeltaToExisting
members.description:
Apply the form's edited values to the LOADED existing graph through the model's
TYPED accessors. `formGraph` is shacl-form's `toRDF()` output (only the shaped
node's triples); `existing` is the loaded resource graph (every existing triple
intact). Read the edited values from `formGraph` via the model's `parse*`, then
write them onto `existing` via the model's typed setters — so only the shape's
predicates change, dual-predicate writes happen, and untouched triples survive.
MUST NOT hand-build a quad. Mutates `existing` in place (return is optional).

members.name: src
members.description: The resource URL to edit. Setting it (re)renders the bound form.

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch (used for BOTH the read + the §10 write).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The credential-free fetch for a public/foreign DATA read (rare for an editor).

members.name: base
members.description:
The base URL writes are confined to (the DataWriter scope guard). Defaults to
the resource's own directory when unset, so a save can never leave the edited
resource's container.

members.name: resolveOptions
members.description: Resolver options forwarded to the §9 pre-fetch (max bytes / timeout / test stub).

members.name: mergeSaveCallback
members.description:
The §10 merge-save callback handed to <jeswr-shacl-form>. Builds a DataWriter
scoped to `base` (or the resource directory) and runs `saveMerged`, whose mutator
delegates to the subclass's applyFormDeltaToExisting on the LOADED graph.

members.name: dataSource
members.description: Build the data-graph source for the inner form: the resource, read with `fetch`.

members.name: #onChildState
members.description: Forward a child <jeswr-shacl-form>'s save state up so this element can reflect it.

members.name: save
members.description: Imperatively trigger a save on the inner editable form.

members.name: #onSave
members.description: Re-emit the inner form's save as this element's own event + mirror the state.

members.name: loadFrom
members.description:
Read this element's resource through the controller and return the parsed graph
plus the base URL its subjects resolve against. A single-resource element calls
`controller.read`; a container element calls `controller.listContainer`.

members.name: renderReady
members.description: Render the parsed model once `status === "ready"` (the subject graph in `graph`).

members.name: src
members.description: The resource / container URL to read. Setting it (re)reads through the seam.

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch (the user's origin).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The credential-free fetch for foreign/public reads (no fallback — see DataSeam).

members.name: publicRead
members.description: Read with the public (credential-free) fetch — for a foreign-origin `src`.

members.name: store
members.description:
A pre-parsed n3 Store to render directly, bypassing the network. When set it
takes precedence over `src` for the NEXT render. (The codegen/test seam — render
a graph already in hand with no fetch.) NOTE: deliberately named `store`, NOT
`dataset`, because `HTMLElement.dataset` is a reserved DOM property (a
`DOMStringMap`); shadowing it with an n3 Store would break the element type.

members.name: #readToken
members.description: A monotonically increasing token to drop the result of a superseded read.

members.name: inputProps
members.description: The input prop names this element re-reads on. Override to extend the base set.

members.name: #applyDirectStore
members.description: Render the directly-set `store` (no network), or fall back to idle when cleared.

members.name: #read
members.description: Read `src` through a DataController, classify any failure, drop a superseded result.

declarations.description:
A generic LDP container listing element.

(No `@solid-class` for a NAMED model class: it binds the generic `ldp:Container` /
`ldp:BasicContainer` — declared in the resolver map as the lowest-priority fallback
— rather than a domain class. Advertised here so the CEM still records the binding.)

members.name: typeIndex
members.description:
Optional injected Solid Type Index registrations, so a child container that is a
registered `solid:instanceContainer` is labelled by its class. Phase-1 does not
fetch the index itself (a documented follow-up); a consumer with the registrations
passes them here.

declarations.name: BASE_FORM_INPUT_PROPS
declarations.description: The input props the base re-renders the editable form on.

Base class for the per-class editable form elements. It mounts the inner
<jeswr-shacl-form> (the §9-hardened editable wrapper) bound to the model's shape +
the resource at `src`, and wires the §10 merge save. A subclass implements:
  - shapeTurtle: the model's SHACL shape, as inline Turtle.
  - applyFormDeltaToExisting: read the edited values from the form graph
    (via the model's `parse*`) and apply them to the LOADED existing graph (via the
    model's typed setters). MUST go through the model's typed accessors — no quad
    hand-built. Mutates the existing graph in place (or returns a Store).
declarations.name: AbstractFormElement

the resource's own directory when unset, so a save can never leave the edited
resource's container.

declarations.name: defaultBaseFor
declarations.description:
The default DataWriter scope base for a resource: its containing directory (the
path up to + including the last `/`). So a save of `…/tasks/1` is confined to
`…/tasks/`. Falls back to the resource origin on a parse failure (still
same-origin-confined). Pure; no network.

declarations.name: findEditedSubject

parameters.name: typeIri
parameters.description: the model class IRI (wf:Task / vcard:Individual / book:Bookmark).

parameters.name: namedNode
parameters.description: the n3 DataFactory.namedNode (passed in by the subclass).

declarations.description:
Find the EDITED subject in shacl-form's `toRDF()` form graph — the node whose
field values the merge reads. shacl-form in EDIT mode binds the form to a subject
that is NOT necessarily the resource's conventional subject (it commonly MINTS a
fresh IRI, observed under the real upstream), so we must locate the form's typed
subject rather than assume `${url}#it`. Returns the first NamedNode subject typed
with `typeIri` in the form graph, preferring `conventional` if IT is the typed one,
else `conventional` as a last resort (an empty form graph). A direct quad scan
(existence query — no triple built), via a `namedNode` factory so the n3 term
construction lives at the call site (each form imports n3's DataFactory once).

IMPORTANT — this is the READ subject (in the form graph). The merge WRITES onto the
resource's `conventional` subject in the EXISTING graph (so the saved triples land
on `${url}#it`/`#this`, not on shacl-form's minted IRI). See each per-class form.

A read-only WebID profile card.

(No `@solid-class`: a WebID profile has no single canonical `rdf:type` — it is
identified by being a `solid:oidcIssuer`-bearing / `foaf:Person` subject — so this
element is bound by IRI, not auto-resolved by `<solid-view>` on rdf:type. It is the
composition target a contact's WebID link or an explicit `src` points at.)
declarations.name: JeswrProfileCard

members.name: shapes
members.description: The SHACL shapes graph source. Required before anything renders.

members.name: values
members.description: The data graph source to edit against the shapes. Required to render.

members.name: shapeSubject
members.description: Optionally pin which node shape to edit (shacl-form's `data-shape-subject`).

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch (for `trusted`+`auth` sources).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The pristine credential-free fetch (for `trusted`+`public` sources).

members.name: resolveOptions
members.description: Resolver options forwarded to the pre-fetch (max bytes / timeout / test stub).

members.name: mergeSave
members.description:
The §10 merge-save callback. When set, JeswrShaclForm.save delegates to
it (the per-class forms wire it to a DataWriter merge). When UNSET, `save()`
throws — this base element refuses to do a naive write itself.

members.name: showSaveButton
members.description: Whether to render the built-in save button (default true).

members.name: #renderToken
members.description: A monotonically increasing token to drop the result of a superseded resolve.

members.name: #saveToken
members.description: A monotonically increasing token so a stale save can't flip a newer one's state.

members.name: #resolve
members.description:
Pre-fetch + §9-harden both graphs through the SHARED pipeline (the SAME one the
read view uses). Fail-closed: empty shapes / any error → the error view with no
partially-applied inline graph, so a mounted <shacl-form> never sees bad input.

members.name: save
members.description:
SAVE — the §10 merge write. Reads the edited graph from shacl-form (`toRDF()` —
only the shaped node's triples), runs an ADVISORY client validation (warn,
never block), then delegates the actual write to JeswrShaclForm.mergeSave
(the per-class forms wire a DataWriter §10 merge). Optimistic state:
saving → saved on success, → error + a surfaced message on failure (revert).

members.name: #emit
members.description: Fire a CustomEvent (composed so a consuming app outside the light DOM hears it).

members.name: #renderActions
members.description: The save button + the saving/saved/error indicator.

members.name: #statusIndicator
members.description: The non-button saving/saved/error text (escaped — Lit text interpolation).

attributes.name: shape-subject
attributes.description: Optionally pin which node shape to edit (shacl-form's `data-shape-subject`).

attributes.name: show-save-button
attributes.description: Whether to render the built-in save button (default true).

members.name: shapes
members.description: The SHACL shapes graph source. Required before anything renders.

members.name: values
members.description: The data graph source to render against the shapes. Required to render.

members.name: shapeSubject
members.description:
Optionally pin which node shape to render (shacl-form's `data-shape-subject`).
A plain string IRI — set on the inner element verbatim, NOT a URL to fetch.

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch (for `trusted`+`auth` sources).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The pristine credential-free fetch (for `trusted`+`public` sources).

members.name: resolveOptions
members.description:
Resolver options forwarded to the pre-fetch (max bytes / timeout / a test
loader stub for guarded-fetch). Never includes a fetch — those come from the
seam properties above so the credential boundary stays explicit.

members.name: #renderToken
members.description: A monotonically increasing token to drop the result of a superseded resolve.

members.name: #resolve
members.description:
Pre-fetch + serialise both graphs, then drop them into state so render()
inlines them onto <shacl-form>. Fail-closed: any error → the error view, with
no partially-applied inline graph.

attributes.name: shape-subject
attributes.description:
Optionally pin which node shape to render (shacl-form's `data-shape-subject`).
A plain string IRI — set on the inner element verbatim, NOT a URL to fetch.

declarations.name: CONTACT_SHAPE_TTL
declarations.default: ` @prefix sh: <http://www.w3.org/ns/shacl#> . @prefix vcard: <http://www.w3.org/2006/vcard/ns#> . @prefix xsd: <http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#> . [] a sh:NodeShape ; sh:targetClass vcard:Individual ; sh:property [ sh:path vcard:fn ; sh:name "Name" ; sh:order 1 ; sh:datatype xsd:string ; sh:minCount 1 ; sh:maxCount 1 ] ; sh:property [ sh:path vcard:organization-name ; sh:name "Organisation" ; sh:order 2 ; sh:datatype xsd:string ; sh:maxCount 1 ] ; sh:property [ sh:path vcard:note ; sh:name "Note" ; sh:order 3 ; sh:datatype xsd:string ; sh:maxCount 1 ] . `
declarations.description: `vcard:fn` etc. — the editable `vcard:Individual` shape (FLAT string fields).

declarations.name: BASE_INPUT_PROPS
declarations.description:
The set of property names whose change should (re)trigger a read. A subclass
EXTENDS this list (spread it, never duplicate it) so it inherits every base input
and cannot silently drift. The base watches `src` / `fetch` / `publicFetch` /
`publicRead`: `publicRead` MUST be here — it selects which fetch (`publicFetch` vs
the authed `fetch`) `loadFrom` reads with, so toggling `public-read` after the
initial load has to re-read through the now-correct credential path.

Base class for the read-only data-binding elements. Holds the DataController seam,
the load lifecycle (idle/loading/ready/error with a supersede token), and the
shared render branches (loading / error / empty). A subclass implements
AbstractReadElement.loadFrom (how to read its resource — `read` vs
`listContainer`) and AbstractReadElement.renderReady (how to render the
parsed model).

Drive it imperatively (the codegen-friendly path):

  const el = document.createElement("jeswr-task-list");
  el.fetch = session.fetch;        // the user's authenticated fetch
  el.src = "https://alice.example/tasks/";
  document.body.append(el);

…or render a pre-parsed graph with no network (tests / SSR-of-a-cached-graph):

  el.dataset = store;              // an n3 Store already in hand
declarations.name: AbstractReadElement

members.name: src
members.description: The resource / container URL to read. Setting it (re)reads through the seam.

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch (the user's origin).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The credential-free fetch for foreign/public reads (no fallback — see DataSeam).

members.name: publicRead
members.description: Read with the public (credential-free) fetch — for a foreign-origin `src`.

members.name: store
members.description:
A pre-parsed n3 Store to render directly, bypassing the network. When set it
takes precedence over `src` for the NEXT render. (The codegen/test seam — render
a graph already in hand with no fetch.) NOTE: deliberately named `store`, NOT
`dataset`, because `HTMLElement.dataset` is a reserved DOM property (a
`DOMStringMap`); shadowing it with an n3 Store would break the element type.

members.name: #readToken
members.description: A monotonically increasing token to drop the result of a superseded read.

members.name: inputProps
members.description: The input prop names this element re-reads on. Override to extend the base set.

members.name: #applyDirectStore
members.description: Render the directly-set `store` (no network), or fall back to idle when cleared.

members.name: #read
members.description: Read `src` through a DataController, classify any failure, drop a superseded result.

members.name: loadFrom
members.description:
Read this element's resource through the controller and return the parsed graph
plus the base URL its subjects resolve against. A single-resource element calls
`controller.read`; a container element calls `controller.listContainer`.

members.name: renderReady
members.description: Render the parsed model once `status === "ready"` (the subject graph in `graph`).

attributes.name: src
attributes.description: The resource / container URL to read. Setting it (re)reads through the seam.

attributes.name: public-read
attributes.description: Read with the public (credential-free) fetch — for a foreign-origin `src`.

declarations.name: safeHref
declarations.description:
An http(s)-only href filter for any IRI bound to an `<a href>`. Pod data is
untrusted: a `javascript:` / `data:` value must NEVER reach an href. Returns the
IRI when it is a well-formed http(s) URL, else `undefined` (the caller renders the
value as escaped TEXT instead of a link). Mirrors the data models' http(s) filter.

declarations.name: safeMailto
declarations.description:
A `mailto:` filter for an email href. The contact model already returns canonical
`mailto:` IRIs, but this is the belt-and-braces filter at the DOM boundary so a
malformed value renders as text, not a link.

declarations.name: safeTel
declarations.description: A `tel:` filter for a phone href, same belt-and-braces rationale as safeMailto.

declarations.name: stripScheme
declarations.description: Strip a leading `mailto:` / `tel:` scheme for display text (the raw address).

declarations.name: formatDate
declarations.description: Format a Date for display, or empty string for undefined. Locale-default, date only.

members.name: src
members.description: The resource URL to render. Setting it re-resolves.

members.name: classIri
members.description:
Optionally PIN the RDF class to render as, skipping the type probe entirely (the
codegen "I know it's a wf:Task" path). A plain class IRI string. When set, the
resolver maps it directly and no network probe is done before mount.

members.name: mode
members.description: Constrain resolution to a mode (Phase-1 is always `view`).

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch.

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The credential-free fetch for foreign/public reads (no fallback — see DataSeam).

members.name: publicRead
members.description: Probe + read with the public (credential-free) fetch — for a foreign-origin `src`.

members.name: #token
members.description: A supersede token so a stale probe never mounts over a newer one.

members.name: #applyResolution
members.description: Apply a resolution: lazy-load + mount the element, or fall back to unsupported.

members.name: #ensureRegistered
members.description: Lazy-import the element's module so its `customElements.define` has run.

members.name: #renderResolved
members.description: Mount the resolved child element, forwarding the seam + src as properties.

declarations.description: An editable `wf:Task` form element.
declarations.name: JeswrTaskForm

members.name: shapeTurtle
members.description: The model's SHACL shape graph as inline Turtle.

members.name: applyFormDeltaToExisting
members.description:
Apply the edited task fields from the form graph onto the existing graph, via the
model's typed `Task` accessor on each. Reads through `new Task(readSubject,
formGraph)` (the form's edited node, which shacl-form may have minted) and writes
through `new Task(writeSubject, existing)` (the resource's conventional `#it`) —
so the saved triples land on `${url}#it` regardless of shacl-form's minted IRI.
Only the shape's predicates change; the `description` setter writes BOTH
wf:description + dct:description (the dual-predicate contract); every untouched
triple on `existing` (and on OTHER subjects) is preserved.

members.name: src
members.description: The resource URL to edit. Setting it (re)renders the bound form.

members.name: fetch
members.description: The session-bound authenticated fetch (used for BOTH the read + the §10 write).

members.name: publicFetch
members.description: The credential-free fetch for a public/foreign DATA read (rare for an editor).

members.name: base
members.description:
The base URL writes are confined to (the DataWriter scope guard). Defaults to
the resource's own directory when unset, so a save can never leave the edited
resource's container.

members.name: resolveOptions
members.description: Resolver options forwarded to the §9 pre-fetch (max bytes / timeout / test stub).

members.name: mergeSaveCallback
members.description:
The §10 merge-save callback handed to <jeswr-shacl-form>. Builds a DataWriter
scoped to `base` (or the resource directory) and runs `saveMerged`, whose mutator
delegates to the subclass's applyFormDeltaToExisting on the LOADED graph.

members.name: dataSource
members.description: Build the data-graph source for the inner form: the resource, read with `fetch`.

members.name: #onChildState
members.description: Forward a child <jeswr-shacl-form>'s save state up so this element can reflect it.

members.name: save
members.description: Imperatively trigger a save on the inner editable form.

members.name: #onSave
members.description: Re-emit the inner form's save as this element's own event + mirror the state.

attributes.name: src
attributes.description: The resource URL to edit. Setting it (re)renders the bound form.

attributes.name: base
attributes.description:
The base URL writes are confined to (the DataWriter scope guard). Defaults to
the resource's own directory when unset, so a save can never leave the edited
resource's container.

declarations.description:
A read-only `wf:Task` list element.

(No `@solid-shape`: the model's SHACL shape is an anonymous `sh:NodeShape` with a
`sh:targetClass wf:Task`, so there is no canonical shape IRI to advertise. The
`@solid-class` target class is the binding key the resolver maps on.)
declarations.name: JeswrTaskList

cssParts.description: A task's metadata row (assignee / due / priority).
cssParts.name: meta

cssParts.description: Placeholder when the graph holds no tasks.
cssParts.name: empty

cssParts.description: The error message when the read fails.
